fungal infection: Disease: fungal infection. ICD-10: B49. First-line treatment: Fluconazole. Dose: 150mg single dose (oral) or 200mg once daily for 7–14 days. Drug class: Azole antifungal. Reasoning: Fluconazole is the first-line treatment for most superficial fungal infections. It inhibits ergosterol synthesis disrupting fungal cell membrane integrity.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Clotrimazole topical, Itraconazole. Monitoring: Liver function tests with prolonged use.
allergy: Disease: allergy. ICD-10: T78.4. First-line treatment: Cetirizine. Dose: 10mg once daily (oral). Drug class: Second-generation antihistamine. Reasoning: Second-generation antihistamines are first-line for allergic symptoms. Cetirizine selectively blocks H1 receptors with minimal sedation.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Loratadine 10mg, Fexofenadine 180mg. Monitoring: None required for short-term use.
gerd: Disease: gerd. ICD-10: K21.0. First-line treatment: Omeprazole. Dose: 20mg once daily before breakfast for 4–8 weeks. Drug class: Proton pump inhibitor (PPI). Reasoning: PPIs are the most effective agents for acid suppression in GERD. Omeprazole irreversibly inhibits H+/K+ ATPase in gastric parietal cells.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Esomeprazole 40mg, Pantoprazole 40mg. Monitoring: Magnesium levels with long-term use; B12 levels.
chronic cholestasis: Disease: chronic cholestasis. ICD-10: K83.1. First-line treatment: Ursodeoxycholic acid (UDCA). Dose: 13–15 mg/kg/day in 2–3 divided doses. Drug class: Bile acid. Reasoning: UDCA replaces toxic bile acids, reduces hepatocyte injury, and improves bile flow in cholestatic liver disease.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Cholestyramine for pruritus relief. Monitoring: LFTs every 6 months.
drug reaction: Disease: drug reaction. ICD-10: T88.7. First-line treatment: Discontinue causative agent + Prednisolone. Dose: Prednisolone 1 mg/kg/day tapered over 2 weeks. Drug class: Corticosteroid (if needed for severe reaction). Reasoning: Immediate withdrawal of the offending drug is mandatory. Systemic corticosteroids are used for severe cutaneous reactions (e.g., DRESS, SJS).. Evidence level: B. Alternative: Antihistamines for mild urticaria. Monitoring: Blood glucose, electrolytes during steroid course.
peptic ulcer disease: Disease: peptic ulcer disease. ICD-10: K27.9. First-line treatment: Omeprazole + Amoxicillin + Clarithromycin. Dose: Omeprazole 20mg BD + Amoxicillin 1g BD + Clarithromycin 500mg BD for 7–14 days (if H. pylori positive). Drug class: PPI + antibiotics (triple therapy). Reasoning: H. pylori eradication is first-line for peptic ulcer disease. Triple therapy with PPI and two antibiotics achieves >85% eradication rates.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Bismuth quadruple therapy if clarithromycin-resistant region. Monitoring: Repeat H. pylori test 4 weeks post-treatment.
aids: Disease: aids. ICD-10: B24. First-line treatment: Tenofovir + Emtricitabine + Dolutegravir. Dose: TDF 300mg + FTC 200mg + DTG 50mg once daily. Drug class: Antiretroviral therapy (ART) — Integrase inhibitor-based regimen. Reasoning: WHO-recommended first-line ART. Dolutegravir has high barrier to resistance, excellent tolerability, and once-daily dosing.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Efavirenz-based regimen if DTG unavailable. Monitoring: CD4 count, viral load every 6 months; renal function (TDF).
diabetes: Disease: diabetes. ICD-10: E11.9. First-line treatment: Metformin. Dose: 500mg twice daily with meals, titrate to 1000mg twice daily. Drug class: Biguanide. Reasoning: Metformin is first-line for Type 2 Diabetes (ADA guidelines). It reduces hepatic glucose production, improves insulin sensitivity, and is weight-neutral.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: SGLT-2 inhibitors (Empagliflozin) if CVD present. Monitoring: HbA1c every 3–6 months; eGFR annually; B12 levels.
gastroenteritis: Disease: gastroenteritis. ICD-10: A09. First-line treatment: Oral Rehydration Solution (ORS). Dose: 200–400ml after each loose stool. Drug class: Electrolyte replacement. Reasoning: Viral gastroenteritis is self-limiting. ORS prevents dehydration. Antibiotics not indicated unless bacterial cause confirmed (Salmonella, Shigella).. Evidence level: A. Alternative: IV fluids if severe dehydration; Ciprofloxacin for bacterial GE. Monitoring: Hydration status; electrolytes if severe.
bronchial asthma: Disease: bronchial asthma. ICD-10: J45.9. First-line treatment: Salbutamol (short-acting bronchodilator) + Budesonide (ICS). Dose: Salbutamol 100–200mcg inhaled PRN; Budesonide 200–400mcg BD. Drug class: SABA + Inhaled corticosteroid. Reasoning: GINA guidelines recommend ICS as first-line controller therapy. SABAs provide rapid bronchodilation for acute symptoms.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Montelukast as add-on; LABA + ICS if uncontrolled. Monitoring: Peak flow monitoring; annual spirometry.
hypertension: Disease: hypertension. ICD-10: I10. First-line treatment: Amlodipine. Dose: 5mg once daily, titrate to 10mg if BP uncontrolled. Drug class: Calcium channel blocker (CCB). Reasoning: CCBs and ACE inhibitors are first-line per JNC 8 / ESC guidelines. Amlodipine has long half-life, good tolerability, and reduces CV events.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Lisinopril 10mg OD; Hydrochlorothiazide 12.5–25mg OD. Monitoring: BP every 4 weeks until target; electrolytes; renal function.
migraine: Disease: migraine. ICD-10: G43.9. First-line treatment: Sumatriptan. Dose: 50–100mg oral at onset; may repeat after 2h (max 200mg/24h). Drug class: 5-HT1 agonist (Triptan). Reasoning: Triptans are the most effective abortive therapy for moderate-to-severe migraine. They cause cranial vasoconstriction and inhibit CGRP release.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Naproxen 500mg for mild attacks; Topiramate for prevention. Monitoring: Cardiovascular status before use (Triptans contraindicated in CVD).
cervical spondylosis: Disease: cervical spondylosis. ICD-10: M47.812. First-line treatment: Ibuprofen. Dose: 400–600mg three times daily with food for 5–7 days. Drug class: NSAID. Reasoning: NSAIDs reduce inflammation and pain in cervical spondylosis. Physiotherapy is first-line non-pharmacological treatment.. Evidence level: B. Alternative: Paracetamol 1g TDS; Muscle relaxants (Cyclobenzaprine) for spasm. Monitoring: Renal function; GI symptoms with prolonged use.
paralysis (brain hemorrhage): Disease: paralysis (brain hemorrhage). ICD-10: I61.9. First-line treatment: Supportive care + Nimodipine (subarachnoid type) + Antihypertensives. Dose: Nimodipine 60mg every 4h for 21 days (subarachnoid hemorrhage). Drug class: Calcium channel blocker (neuroprotective). Reasoning: Acute intracerebral hemorrhage requires blood pressure control, reversal of anticoagulation if applicable, and ICP management. Nimodipine prevents vasospasm in SAH.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Labetalol for acute BP control; Neurosurgical referral. Monitoring: Neurological status; Glasgow Coma Scale; CT imaging.
jaundice: Disease: jaundice. ICD-10: R17. First-line treatment: Treat underlying cause (e.g., UDCA for cholestasis, antivirals for hepatitis). Dose: Varies by etiology. Drug class: Varies by cause. Reasoning: Jaundice is a symptom, not a diagnosis. Management requires identifying the underlying cause: pre-hepatic (haemolysis), hepatic (hepatitis, cirrhosis), or post-hepatic (biliary obstruction).. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Phototherapy for neonatal jaundice. Monitoring: Bilirubin levels (total/direct/indirect); LFTs; imaging.
malaria: Disease: malaria. ICD-10: B54. First-line treatment: Artemether-Lumefantrine (Coartem). Dose: 80mg/480mg BD for 3 days (weight-based dosing). Drug class: Artemisinin-based combination therapy (ACT). Reasoning: WHO recommends ACTs as first-line for uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria. For severe malaria, IV Artesunate is preferred.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: IV Artesunate for severe malaria; Chloroquine for P. vivax (if sensitive). Monitoring: Parasitaemia on blood film; CBC; renal function.
chicken pox: Disease: chicken pox. ICD-10: B01.9. First-line treatment: Aciclovir. Dose: 800mg five times daily for 7 days (adult); 20mg/kg QID for 5 days (child). Drug class: Antiviral — nucleoside analogue. Reasoning: Aciclovir reduces duration and severity of varicella if started within 24h of rash onset. Most effective in immunocompromised patients.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Supportive care (antihistamines, calamine) for mild cases. Monitoring: Secondary bacterial infection; renal function (IV dosing).
dengue: Disease: dengue. ICD-10: A90. First-line treatment: Supportive care — Paracetamol + ORS. Dose: Paracetamol 1g every 6h (max 4g/day); ORS as needed. Drug class: Antipyretic + Electrolyte replacement. Reasoning: No specific antiviral exists for dengue. NSAIDs and Aspirin are CONTRAINDICATED due to bleeding risk. Paracetamol controls fever safely.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: IV fluid resuscitation for dengue haemorrhagic fever. Monitoring: Platelet count daily; haematocrit; warning signs.
typhoid: Disease: typhoid. ICD-10: A01.0. First-line treatment: Ceftriaxone. Dose: 2g IV/IM once daily for 10–14 days. Drug class: Third-generation cephalosporin. Reasoning: Parenteral third-generation cephalosporins are preferred for severe typhoid. Oral alternatives: Azithromycin 1g loading then 500mg OD × 7 days.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Azithromycin for uncomplicated typhoid; Ciprofloxacin where susceptibility confirmed. Monitoring: Blood cultures; fever curve; liver function.
hepatitis a: Disease: hepatitis a. ICD-10: B15.9. First-line treatment: Supportive care (rest, nutrition, hydration). Dose: No specific antiviral treatment. Drug class: Supportive. Reasoning: Hepatitis A is self-limiting in most cases. Avoid hepatotoxic drugs (alcohol, paracetamol excess). Vaccination prevents infection.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Hospital admission for fulminant hepatitis. Monitoring: LFTs weekly; INR; bilirubin.
hepatitis b: Disease: hepatitis b. ICD-10: B18.1. First-line treatment: Tenofovir disoproxil fumarate (TDF). Dose: 300mg once daily. Drug class: Nucleotide reverse transcriptase inhibitor. Reasoning: TDF is first-line for chronic Hepatitis B per EASL/AASLD guidelines. High barrier to resistance; can be used in pregnancy.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Entecavir 0.5mg OD; Pegylated interferon for HBeAg+ with high ALT. Monitoring: HBV DNA every 3 months; LFTs; HBsAg/HBeAg status.
hepatitis c: Disease: hepatitis c. ICD-10: B18.2. First-line treatment: Sofosbuvir + Velpatasvir (Epclusa). Dose: 400mg/100mg once daily for 12 weeks. Drug class: Direct-acting antiviral (DAA) — pan-genotypic. Reasoning: DAAs achieve sustained virologic response (SVR) >95% in all genotypes. Pan-genotypic regimens eliminate need for genotyping.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Glecaprevir/Pibrentasvir 8 weeks (non-cirrhotic). Monitoring: HCV RNA at week 4 and 12 weeks post-treatment.
hepatitis d: Disease: hepatitis d. ICD-10: B17.0. First-line treatment: Pegylated Interferon alfa-2a. Dose: 180mcg subcutaneous injection weekly for 48 weeks. Drug class: Immunomodulator / Antiviral. Reasoning: Pegylated IFN-alfa is the only proven treatment for HDV. Bulevirtide (entry inhibitor) is newly approved in Europe.. Evidence level: B. Alternative: Bulevirtide 2mg SC daily (EMA-approved). Monitoring: HDV RNA; ALT; neuropsychiatric side effects.
hepatitis e: Disease: hepatitis e. ICD-10: B17.2. First-line treatment: Supportive care (rest, hydration). Dose: No specific antiviral for immunocompetent patients. Drug class: Supportive. Reasoning: Hepatitis E is self-limiting in immunocompetent patients. Ribavirin is used in immunocompromised/transplant patients.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Ribavirin 600–800mg/day for 12 weeks in immunocompromised. Monitoring: LFTs; INR; extra care in pregnancy (high mortality).
alcoholic hepatitis: Disease: alcoholic hepatitis. ICD-10: K70.1. First-line treatment: Prednisolone. Dose: 40mg once daily for 28 days (Maddrey Discriminant Function >32). Drug class: Corticosteroid. Reasoning: Prednisolone reduces short-term mortality in severe alcoholic hepatitis. Calculate Maddrey DF first; contraindicated if active infection or GI bleed.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Pentoxifylline 400mg TDS if steroids contraindicated. Monitoring: Lille score at day 7; blood glucose; infection surveillance.
tuberculosis: Disease: tuberculosis. ICD-10: A15.9. First-line treatment: HRZE regimen — Isoniazid + Rifampicin + Pyrazinamide + Ethambutol. Dose: Intensive phase: 2 months HRZE; Continuation phase: 4 months HR. Drug class: Combination antibiotic regimen. Reasoning: Standard 6-month DOTS regimen per WHO guidelines. Never use monotherapy — combination prevents resistance. Vitamin B6 (Pyridoxine) co-administered to prevent INH neuropathy.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Bedaquiline + Linezolid for MDR-TB. Monitoring: LFTs monthly; visual acuity (Ethambutol); sputum AFB monthly.
common cold: Disease: common cold. ICD-10: J00. First-line treatment: Supportive care — Paracetamol + Nasal saline + Fluids. Dose: Paracetamol 500mg–1g every 4–6h PRN. Drug class: Antipyretic / Symptomatic relief. Reasoning: Common cold is self-limiting (rhinovirus). Antibiotics are not indicated. Zinc lozenges may reduce duration if started within 24h.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Pseudoephedrine for congestion; Dextromethorphan for cough. Monitoring: Secondary bacterial infection (sinusitis, otitis media).
pneumonia: Disease: pneumonia. ICD-10: J18.9. First-line treatment: Amoxicillin. Dose: 1g three times daily for 5–7 days (community-acquired, outpatient). Drug class: Beta-lactam antibiotic. Reasoning: Streptococcus pneumoniae is the most common CAP pathogen. Amoxicillin covers typical organisms. Azithromycin added for atypical cover if needed.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Azithromycin + Ceftriaxone for hospitalised patients; Levofloxacin as monotherapy. Monitoring: Oxygen saturation; CXR at 6 weeks; blood cultures before antibiotics.
dimorphic hemorrhoids (piles): Disease: dimorphic hemorrhoids (piles). ICD-10: K64.9. First-line treatment: Hydrocortisone + Lidocaine topical cream. Dose: Apply to affected area 2–4 times daily for 5–7 days. Drug class: Topical corticosteroid + local anaesthetic. Reasoning: First-line for symptomatic relief. High-fibre diet and adequate hydration are essential non-pharmacological interventions.. Evidence level: B. Alternative: Rubber band ligation for grade II–III; Haemorrhoidectomy for severe cases. Monitoring: Duration of topical steroid use; surgical referral criteria.
heart attack: Disease: heart attack. ICD-10: I21.9. First-line treatment: Aspirin + Clopidogrel + Atorvastatin + Early PCI/thrombolysis. Dose: Aspirin 300mg loading + Clopidogrel 300mg loading; Atorvastatin 80mg OD. Drug class: Antiplatelets + Statin — acute STEMI protocol. Reasoning: STEMI management: immediate aspirin, dual antiplatelet therapy, statin, and reperfusion strategy within 90 minutes (primary PCI preferred).. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Thrombolysis (Alteplase) if PCI unavailable within 120 min. Monitoring: Cardiac biomarkers (Troponin); ECG monitoring; LVEF echocardiography.
varicose veins: Disease: varicose veins. ICD-10: I83.90. First-line treatment: Compression stockings + Diosmin. Dose: Diosmin 1000mg once daily; Compression class II (23–32 mmHg). Drug class: Venotonic + External compression. Reasoning: Compression reduces venous hypertension. Diosmin improves venous tone and reduces inflammation. Endovenous laser ablation for significant disease.. Evidence level: B. Alternative: Sclerotherapy; Endovenous laser ablation (EVLA). Monitoring: Duplex ultrasound; skin changes.
hypothyroidism: Disease: hypothyroidism. ICD-10: E03.9. First-line treatment: Levothyroxine (L-T4). Dose: 1.6 mcg/kg/day, starting at 25–50mcg OD and titrating every 4–6 weeks. Drug class: Thyroid hormone replacement. Reasoning: Levothyroxine is the gold standard. Dose titrated to achieve TSH in normal range (0.4–4.0 mIU/L). Take 30–60 min before food.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Add T3 (Liothyronine) for persistent symptoms despite normal TSH. Monitoring: TSH every 4–6 weeks until stable; then annually; lipid profile.
hyperthyroidism: Disease: hyperthyroidism. ICD-10: E05.90. First-line treatment: Carbimazole. Dose: 20–40mg once daily initially; reduce to 5–15mg maintenance. Drug class: Thionamide antithyroid drug. Reasoning: Carbimazole blocks thyroid peroxidase, reducing T3/T4 synthesis. Used for Graves' disease. Propranolol added for symptomatic control (tachycardia, tremor).. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Radioactive iodine (I-131); Thyroidectomy. Monitoring: TFTs every 4–6 weeks; CBC (agranulocytosis risk).
hypoglycemia: Disease: hypoglycemia. ICD-10: E16.0. First-line treatment: Oral glucose (if conscious) or Glucagon / IV Dextrose (if unconscious). Dose: 15–20g fast-acting glucose (glucose tablets or juice); IV 50ml of 50% dextrose. Drug class: Glucose replacement. Reasoning: Immediate glucose correction is essential. Follow 15-15 rule: 15g glucose, recheck BG after 15 minutes, repeat if still low.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: IM Glucagon 1mg if IV access not available. Monitoring: Blood glucose every 15 minutes until normalised.
osteoarthritis: Disease: osteoarthritis. ICD-10: M19.90. First-line treatment: Paracetamol. Dose: 1g up to four times daily (max 4g/day). Drug class: Analgesic. Reasoning: Paracetamol is preferred first-line as NSAIDs carry GI/cardiovascular risks. Topical diclofenac is effective for knee OA with reduced systemic risk.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Topical Diclofenac gel; Oral NSAIDs if insufficient; Intra-articular corticosteroid. Monitoring: Pain VAS score; NSAID use — GI protection with PPI.
arthritis: Disease: arthritis. ICD-10: M13.9. First-line treatment: Methotrexate (Rheumatoid Arthritis). Dose: 7.5–25mg once weekly (oral or SC) + Folic acid 5mg/week. Drug class: DMARD (Disease-Modifying Antirheumatic Drug). Reasoning: Methotrexate is the anchor DMARD for RA per ACR/EULAR guidelines. Folic acid supplementation reduces toxicity. Folic acid must always be co-prescribed.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Hydroxychloroquine for mild RA; Biologic DMARDs (anti-TNF) for refractory disease. Monitoring: CBC, LFTs every 4–8 weeks; annual chest X-ray.
paroxysmal positional vertigo: Disease: paroxysmal positional vertigo. ICD-10: H81.10. First-line treatment: Epley Manoeuvre (non-pharmacological) + Betahistine. Dose: Betahistine 16mg three times daily. Drug class: Histamine analogue / Vestibular suppressant. Reasoning: The Epley manoeuvre is curative in >80% of BPPV cases. Betahistine improves inner ear blood flow and reduces vestibular neurotransmission.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Prochlorperazine for acute nausea/vomiting. Monitoring: Dix-Hallpike test after treatment.
acne: Disease: acne. ICD-10: L70.0. First-line treatment: Topical Benzoyl Peroxide + Clindamycin. Dose: Benzoyl peroxide 5% gel OD; Clindamycin 1% gel BD. Drug class: Keratolytic + Topical antibiotic. Reasoning: Combination therapy with benzoyl peroxide and antibiotic is more effective than monotherapy and reduces antibiotic resistance.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Oral Doxycycline 100mg OD for moderate-severe; Isotretinoin for severe/nodular acne. Monitoring: Skin response at 8–10 weeks; Isotretinoin liver and lipid monitoring.
urinary tract infection: Disease: urinary tract infection. ICD-10: N39.0. First-line treatment: Nitrofurantoin. Dose: 100mg modified-release twice daily for 5 days (uncomplicated UTI). Drug class: Urinary antibiotic. Reasoning: Nitrofurantoin is first-line for uncomplicated lower UTI (NICE guidelines). Concentrates in urine. Avoid if eGFR <45 or in late pregnancy.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Trimethoprim 200mg BD × 7 days; Fosfomycin 3g single dose. Monitoring: Urine culture and sensitivity; symptoms at day 3.
psoriasis: Disease: psoriasis. ICD-10: L40.0. First-line treatment: Topical Calcipotriol + Betamethasone dipropionate (Xamiol/Dovobet). Dose: Apply once daily for up to 4 weeks; max 100g/week. Drug class: Vitamin D analogue + Topical corticosteroid. Reasoning: Combination calcipotriol + corticosteroid is superior to either agent alone. Calcipotriol slows keratinocyte proliferation; corticosteroid reduces inflammation.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Methotrexate for moderate–severe psoriasis; Biologics (Adalimumab, Secukinumab) for severe disease. Monitoring: PASI score; skin atrophy with prolonged topical steroid use.
impetigo: Disease: impetigo. ICD-10: L01.00. First-line treatment: Topical Fusidic acid. Dose: Apply to affected area 3 times daily for 5–7 days. Drug class: Topical antibiotic. Reasoning: Non-bullous impetigo (S. aureus, S. pyogenes) responds well to topical fusidic acid. Oral antibiotics for extensive/bullous disease.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Topical Mupirocin; Oral Flucloxacillin 500mg QDS for extensive impetigo. Monitoring: Lesion resolution at 5–7 days; glomerulonephritis risk (post-streptococcal).
gastroenteritis (acute): Disease: gastroenteritis (acute). ICD-10: A09. First-line treatment: Oral Rehydration Salts + Zinc (children). Dose: ORS 10ml/kg after each stool; Zinc 20mg OD for 10–14 days (children). Drug class: Electrolyte replacement + Micronutrient. Reasoning: WHO recommends ORS and zinc for childhood gastroenteritis. Zinc reduces duration and severity. Probiotics (Lactobacillus) may reduce duration.. Evidence level: A. Alternative: Azithromycin for confirmed bacterial GE; Metronidazole for Giardia. Monitoring: Hydration status; weight; signs of sepsis.
